shortDescription.text: Move the password to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.
Move the API key to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.
Move the secret/token to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.
Move the database connection URL to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.
Remove sensitive data from log statements. Use structured logging with redaction filters to automatically mask sensitive fields.
Use SHA-256/SHA-512 for integrity checks, or bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for password hashing.

rules.name: Potential SQL injection via string concatenation

shortDescription.text: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings directly.
Set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Strict (or Lax) flags on all cookies. Use __Host- prefix for sensitive cookies.
Validate file type (MIME + extension + magic bytes), enforce size limits, scan for malware, and store uploads outside the webroot.
Implement CSRF protection using tokens (csurf, django.middleware.csrf, @csrf_exempt annotations) or SameSite cookies.
Remove eval() entirely. Use JSON.parse() for data parsing (JS/TS), ast.literal_eval (Python), or a proper expression parser.
Use textContent for plain text, or use a sanitization library (DOMPurify) before inserting HTML. In React, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML unless content is sanitized.
Never disable TLS certificate validation in production. Use proper CA certificates. If using self-signed certs in development, use a CA bundle instead.
Review each suppression directive to ensure it's justified. Add a comment explaining why the suppression is necessary. Remove any that were added simply to silence warnings.
Enforce minimum 8-character passwords with complexity requirements. Use NIST SP 800-63B guidelines. Check against breached password databases (Have I Been Pwned).
Implement rate limiting on login/auth endpoints. Use progressive delays, account lockouts, or CAPTCHA after failed attempts.

rules.name: Nested loops detected — potential O(n²) complexity

shortDescription.text: Consider using hash maps for lookups (O(1)), sorting + binary search, or restructuring the algorithm. If the nested loop is necessary, ensure the inner dataset is bounded.

rules.name: Potential N+1 query pattern (await in loop)

shortDescription.text: Use Promise.all() to parallelize independent operations, or batch database queries (e.g., WHERE id IN (...) instead of per-ID queries).
Add pagination (LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based), filtering (WHERE clauses), and projection (select only needed fields). Default to a reasonable page size.
Use asynchronous file operations (fs.promises.readFile, aiofiles, async File.ReadAllTextAsync) or streaming for large files.
Externalize state to a database, cache (Redis), or message queue. Use const/final/immutable for configuration. Each instance should be stateless.
Use asynchronous alternatives (async/await, promises, non-blocking I/O). Move long-running work to background queues.
Set explicit timeouts on all external calls (e.g., 5-30 seconds). Implement circuit breakers (e.g., using libraries like cockatiel or opossum) for critical dependencies.

Offload CPU-intensive work to worker threads, a job queue (Bull, Celery), or a dedicated compute service. Use async variants of crypto operations (pbkdf2, scrypt). Consider WebAssembly for hot-path computation.
Use cloud storage (S3, Azure Blob, GCS) for persistent files. Use /tmp only for truly temporary data. Accept paths from environment configuration.

Use a structured logging library (pino/winston for JS, logging with dictConfig for Python, slog for Go, serilog for C#, log4j/slf4j for Java, tracing for Rust) that outputs JSON. Include correlation IDs, timestamps, and log levels.
Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) for all connection strings.
Replace with specific types, generics, or constrained types. In TS enable 'noImplicitAny'. In Go use concrete types or type constraints.
Fix the underlying issue instead of suppressing it. If suppression is truly necessary, add a comment explaining why.
Track TODOs as work items in your issue tracker. Resolve FIXMEs and HACKs before merging to main. Set a code quality gate that flags unresolved TODOs.
Catch specific exception types. In Python, use 'except ValueError' (not bare 'except:'). In Java/C#, catch specific exception classes.
Add meaningful alt text describing the image content. Use alt="" only for purely decorative images.
Add onKeyDown or onKeyPress handlers alongside onClick. Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible.

rules.name: Non-semantic element used with ARIA role

shortDescription.text: Use semantic HTML elements (button, a, h1-h6, nav, main) instead of divs with ARIA roles.
Associate each input with a <label> element using for/id, or use aria-label / aria-labelledby.
Add lang attribute: <html lang="en">. Use the appropriate BCP 47 language tag.
If removing outline, provide a visible alternative focus indicator (box-shadow, border, custom :focus-visible styles).

rules.name: Form error not associated with input via ARIA

shortDescription.text: Use aria-describedby to link error messages to inputs, and aria-invalid='true' on invalid inputs.
Use noun-based URLs (e.g., POST /users instead of POST /createUser). Let HTTP methods convey the action.
Explicitly select only the fields needed for the API response. Use DTOs or view models to shape the output.
Implement pagination using limit/offset, cursor-based, or page-based approaches. Include total count and navigation links.
Add API versioning via URL path (/v1/resource), header (X-API-Version), or query parameter.
Adopt a consistent response envelope (e.g., { data, meta, errors }) across all endpoints.

rules.name: Request body used without content-type validation

shortDescription.text: Use body-parsing middleware (express.json()) and validate Content-Type headers. Reject requests with unexpected content types.
Pass sensitive data in request headers (Authorization) or request body, never in URLs or query parameters.
Set explicit timeouts on all network calls. Use AbortController with setTimeout for fetch, or timeout options for HTTP clients.
Throw errors or use graceful shutdown patterns instead. Let the process exit naturally after cleanup. Reserve panics for truly unrecoverable situations.

rules.name: No fallback for failed external call

shortDescription.text: Provide fallback behavior: cached responses, default values, or gracefully degraded features when dependencies fail.
Accept an idempotency key header (Idempotency-Key) and use it to deduplicate write operations.

rules.name: Console logging instead of structured logger

shortDescription.text: Use a structured logging library (winston/pino for JS, logging for Python, slog for Go, serilog for C#, log4j for Java, tracing for Rust) with log levels, timestamps, and correlation IDs.
Use structured log parameters: logger.info('User action', { userId, action }) instead of string concatenation.
Never log sensitive data. Use redaction middleware or mask sensitive fields before logging. Audit all log statements for PII/secrets.
Batch queries outside the loop using WHERE IN clauses, JOINs, or DataLoader patterns. Fetch all needed data in a single query.
Use async/await versions, non-blocking APIs, or spawn blocking work on a separate thread/runtime. Sync I/O is only acceptable at startup.

rules.name: Duplicate fetch calls to same URL

shortDescription.text: Cache responses or deduplicate requests. Use memoization, request coalescing, or an in-memory/distributed cache.
Use dynamic imports (import()) for heavy libraries. Import specific sub-modules (lodash/get instead of lodash). Consider tree-shakeable alternatives.
Build DOM content in a DocumentFragment or string, then insert once. Use virtual DOM frameworks or batch updates.
Always use pagination (limit/offset or cursor-based) when querying collections. Set reasonable default page sizes.

rules.name: Nested loops detected — O(n²) or worse complexity

shortDescription.text: Replace nested loops with hash maps (O(n)) for lookups, pre-sorted data with binary search, or purpose-built data structures. Consider if the algorithm can be flattened.
Store the interval ID and call clearInterval in cleanup/teardown logic (e.g., useEffect cleanup, componentWillUnmount, or process exit handler).
Encrypt PII fields, mask them in logs and UI displays, and ensure they are stored with column-level encryption.
Never log sensitive data. Use redaction/masking utilities to sanitize log output. Audit all log statements.
Use a PCI-compliant payment processor (Stripe, Braintree). Never store, log, or transmit raw card numbers. Tokenize immediately.
Set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Strict on sensitive cookies. Review cookie consent requirements per jurisdiction.

rules.name: Potential cross-border data egress without jurisdiction checks

shortDescription.text: Add egress controls that validate destination jurisdiction, data classification, and lawful transfer conditions before sending data.

rules.name: Replication/backup configuration may violate localization requirements

shortDescription.text: Pin replication and backup targets to approved jurisdictions and document DR geography constraints.

rules.name: PII stored without geographic partitioning indicator

shortDescription.text: Tag PII records with a region/jurisdiction identifier. Use tenant-scoped region routing for multi-tenant systems. Implement database-level partitioning by geography for regulated data.

Log all administrative and destructive operations to a tamper-evident audit trail. Include actor identity, timestamp, operation type, affected resource, and outcome. Store audit logs in a separate, append-only store with retention policies.
Link TODOs to issue tracker tickets (e.g., TODO(#123): ...). Create tracking issues for existing unlinked TODOs.
Add OpenAPI/Swagger annotations or JSDoc comments documenting request body, query params, response schema, and error codes.
Use an i18n library (react-intl, i18next, vue-i18n) and extract strings to translation files.
Use parameterized translation strings with named placeholders: t('greeting', { name }) instead of 'Hello ' + name.
Use Intl.DateTimeFormat and Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware formatting. Never hardcode date patterns.

rules.name: Numeric values formatted without locale awareness

shortDescription.text: Use Intl.NumberFormat for all user-facing numbers: new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { style: 'currency', currency }).format(amount).
Replace deprecated packages: moment->date-fns/luxon, request->node-fetch/axios, underscore->lodash-es or native methods.

rules.name: Shared mutable state in async context

shortDescription.text: Use request-scoped/context-scoped state, atomic operations, or proper synchronization mechanisms instead of shared mutable variables.

rules.name: Potentially missing await on async operation

shortDescription.text: Add await to async operations, or explicitly handle the returned promise with .catch(). Use ESLint's no-floating-promises rule.
For independent operations, collect promises and use Promise.all() (with concurrency limits). Keep sequential only if order matters.
Store the interval ID and call clearInterval in cleanup/unmount/dispose handlers.
Review whether demographic-based logic is legally compliant and ethically justified. Document the business justification. Consider bias testing.
Use inclusive alternatives: allowlist/denylist, primary/replica, placeholder, confidence check.

rules.name: Weak or unsafe type usage detected

shortDescription.text: Replace weak types with specific types: use 'unknown' with type guards (TS), generics (Java/C#), concrete types (Go), safe wrappers (Rust).
Extract magic numbers into named constants (e.g., const HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000). Use enums for related sets of values.
Convert TODO/FIXME comments into tracked issues in your project management tool. Resolve HACK comments with proper implementations.
Use an options object parameter: func({ name, age, ...opts }). This is self-documenting, order-independent, and extensible.

rules.name: Duplicate string literals — extract to constants

shortDescription.text: Extract repeated strings into named constants. This makes updates a single-point change and prevents typos.

rules.name: Abrupt process termination instead of proper error handling

shortDescription.text: Use proper error propagation instead of abrupt termination. Return error responses in HTTP servers. Let the process shutdown gracefully.
Include a machine-readable error code in responses: { code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message: '...' }. Use RFC 7807 Problem Details format.
Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Never commit credentials to version control.

rules.name: Sensitive tokens passed in query parameters

shortDescription.text: Pass tokens in the Authorization header (Bearer scheme) or in httpOnly cookies. Never use query parameters for sensitive credentials.
Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing. These algorithms are intentionally slow and include salt by default.
Never disable TLS verification in production. Fix certificate issues properly. Use CA bundles for self-signed certs in development only.
Set cookies with { secure: true, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict' }. Use Secure for all auth cookies. HttpOnly prevents JavaScript access.
Use parameterized queries (placeholders) or prepared statements. ORMs handle this automatically. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.
Select only the columns you need: SELECT id, name, email FROM users. This reduces network transfer, memory usage, and improves query plan optimization.
Use batch queries (WHERE id IN (...)), JOINs, or ORM eager loading (include/populate) to fetch related data in a single query.
Wrap multi-step data mutations in transactions. Use BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK or ORM transaction APIs to ensure atomicity.
Use environment variables for connection strings. Store credentials in a secrets manager. Use different connection strings per environment.

rules.name: Destructive DDL statements in application code

shortDescription.text: Never run destructive DDL from application code. Use migration tools (Prisma, Flyway, Alembic) with review and rollback support. Require elevated permissions for DDL.

rules.name: Database credentials embedded in connection string

shortDescription.text: Use separate credential parameters or environment variables. Consider IAM/managed identity for passwordless database connections in cloud environments.
Store secrets in a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Inject via environment variables at runtime. Never commit secrets.

rules.name: Configuration values hardcoded instead of externalized

shortDescription.text: Read configuration from environment variables (process.env.PORT). Use a config library (convict, dotenv, django-environ) to validate and provide defaults.

rules.name: .env usage detected — ensure it is not committed

shortDescription.text: Add .env to .gitignore. Create a .env.example with placeholder values documenting required environment variables. Use CI/CD variables for deployment.

rules.name: Multiple response formats — verify contract consistency

shortDescription.text: Use a consistent response envelope across all endpoints. Define response schemas (OpenAPI/Swagger) to enforce contracts.

rules.name: HTTP method mismatch — destructive action via POST

shortDescription.text: Use appropriate HTTP methods: DELETE for removal, PUT/PATCH for updates. If migrating methods, keep the old method working during a deprecation period.
Use platform-independent path construction (path.join, os.path.join, Path.Combine). Use environment variables or config for base directories.

rules.name: File I/O without explicit line-ending handling

shortDescription.text: Use 'utf-8' encoding explicitly. Consider normalizing line endings when reading files. Configure .gitattributes for consistent line endings in version control.
Use cross-platform helpers like os.homedir(), os.tmpdir(), or libraries like 'env-paths' to resolve platform-appropriate directories.

rules.name: Browser-specific APIs used in server-side code

shortDescription.text: Guard browser API usage with typeof checks (e.g., typeof window !== 'undefined'). Use isomorphic libraries for code shared between client and server.
Use addEventListener() or framework event bindings (React onClick, Vue @click). Separate behavior from markup for maintainability and CSP compliance.
Disable the submit button during submission. Show a loading indicator. Prevent double-submission at the application layer.
Add confirmation dialogs for destructive actions. Show what will be affected. Consider soft-delete with undo capability.
Implement pagination (offset-based or cursor-based). Return total count and page info. Enforce maximum page sizes.
Show success notifications (toasts, alerts) after mutations. Provide clear visual feedback. Consider optimistic UI updates with rollback on failure.
Add client-side validation before submission. Use schema validation libraries (Zod, Yup, Joi). Show inline validation feedback. Keep server-side validation as well.
Never log authentication tokens, Authorization headers, or session IDs. If request logging is needed, redact sensitive headers before logging.
Never log passwords, credentials, or secrets. Implement a log sanitizer that redacts sensitive fields automatically.
Use a structured logging library (pino, winston) that supports field-level redaction, log level filtering, and structured output for automated sensitivity scanning.
Use structured logging with named fields: logger.info({ userId, action }, 'User action performed'). This allows automated redaction of specific fields.
Anonymize IP addresses in logs (truncate last octet for IPv4, mask prefix for IPv6). If full IP is needed for security, ensure log retention complies with privacy policy.

rules.name: Database queries logged — may contain sensitive parameters

shortDescription.text: Log query templates without parameter values. Use parameterized query logging that replaces bind values with placeholders. Redact sensitive column values.
Always enforce a maximum result limit: db.find({}).limit(100). Implement pagination and enforce maximum page sizes.
Use setTimeout with re-scheduling instead of setInterval to prevent overlap. Add guards to skip execution if the previous run hasn't completed.

Apply strict rate limits to auth endpoints (e.g., 5-10 requests/minute per IP). Use progressive delays or CAPTCHA after failed attempts. Consider using 'express-rate-limit' or 'rate-limiter-flexible'.

rules.name: File upload without size or count limits

shortDescription.text: Set explicit file size limits (e.g., multer({ limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 } })). Limit the number of files per request. Validate file types.

rules.name: Hard process termination hinders graceful CI/CD lifecycle

shortDescription.text: Use proper error propagation instead of hard exits. In production, handle SIGTERM gracefully. Let the runtime manage process lifecycle.
Fix the underlying issues instead of suppressing them. If suppression is necessary, add a comment explaining why and create a tracking issue to resolve it.
Implement the security controls indicated by each comment before merging. If the control is not needed, remove the comment and document why. Do not ship TODO security comments to production.

rules.name: Type safety bypassed in security-critical code

shortDescription.text: Define proper interfaces for security-related data structures (tokens, sessions, credentials). Replace 'as any' with explicit types or runtime validation (zod, io-ts).
Move all endpoint URLs and IP addresses to environment variables or a configuration file. Use service discovery or DNS for internal services.

Add schema validation for all request inputs (body, query, params). Use zod, joi, or yup (Node.js), pydantic (Python), class-validator (NestJS), or equivalent for your framework. Validate at the boundary before any business logic.
Replace MD5/SHA-1 with SHA-256+ for integrity checks, or bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for password hashing. Use crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data) in web contexts.

Never disable TLS verification in production. Use properly signed certificates (Let's Encrypt is free). If self-signed certificates are required for internal services, configure the specific CA certificate rather than disabling all verification.

rules.name: Express error middleware registered before routes

shortDescription.text: Move error-handling middleware to after all route registrations: first register all routes, then app.use(errorHandler).

rules.name: Express app without helmet() — missing security headers

shortDescription.text: Install and use helmet: npm install helmet, then app.use(helmet()). This sets 11 security headers with sensible defaults.

message.text: Hardcoded password detected: Move the password to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.

Hardcoded API key detected: Move the API key to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.

Hardcoded secret/token detected: Move the secret/token to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.

Hardcoded database connection URL detected: Move the database connection URL to a secrets manager (e.g., Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) or at minimum to environment variables. Never commit secrets to source control.
Sensitive data may be logged: Remove sensitive data from log statements. Use structured logging with redaction filters to automatically mask sensitive fields.
Weak hashing algorithm used: Use SHA-256/SHA-512 for integrity checks, or bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for password hashing.
Potential SQL injection via string concatenation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings directly.
Cookie may lack security flags: Set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Strict (or Lax) flags on all cookies. Use __Host- prefix for sensitive cookies.
File upload without type/size validation: Validate file type (MIME + extension + magic bytes), enforce size limits, scan for malware, and store uploads outside the webroot.
No CSRF protection detected: Implement CSRF protection using tokens (csurf, django.middleware.csrf, @csrf_exempt annotations) or SameSite cookies.
Dangerous eval()/exec() usage: Remove eval() entirely. Use JSON.parse() for data parsing (JS/TS), ast.literal_eval (Python), or a proper expression parser.
Potential XSS via innerHTML: Use textContent for plain text, or use a sanitization library (DOMPurify) before inserting HTML. In React, avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML unless content is sanitized.
TLS certificate validation disabled: Never disable TLS certificate validation in production. Use proper CA certificates. If using self-signed certs in development, use a CA bundle instead.

Linter/type-checker suppression directives found: Review each suppression directive to ensure it's justified. Add a comment explaining why the suppression is necessary. Remove any that were added simply to silence warnings.
No password complexity validation: Enforce minimum 8-character passwords with complexity requirements. Use NIST SP 800-63B guidelines. Check against breached password databases (Have I Been Pwned).
Authentication endpoints without rate limiting: Implement rate limiting on login/auth endpoints. Use progressive delays, account lockouts, or CAPTCHA after failed attempts.

Nested loops detected — potential O(n²) complexity: Consider using hash maps for lookups (O(1)), sorting + binary search, or restructuring the algorithm. If the nested loop is necessary, ensure the inner dataset is bounded.
Potential N+1 query pattern (await in loop): Use Promise.all() to parallelize independent operations, or batch database queries (e.g., WHERE id IN (...) instead of per-ID queries).
Unbounded data query: Add pagination (LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based), filtering (WHERE clauses), and projection (select only needed fields). Default to a reasonable page size.
Synchronous/blocking file I/O detected: Use asynchronous file operations (fs.promises.readFile, aiofiles, async File.ReadAllTextAsync) or streaming for large files.
Global mutable state detected: Externalize state to a database, cache (Redis), or message queue. Use const/final/immutable for configuration. Each instance should be stateless.
Synchronous blocking operation: Use asynchronous alternatives (async/await, promises, non-blocking I/O). Move long-running work to background queues.

External calls without timeout: Set explicit timeouts on all external calls (e.g., 5-30 seconds). Implement circuit breakers (e.g., using libraries like cockatiel or opossum) for critical dependencies.

CPU-intensive computation may block scaling: Offload CPU-intensive work to worker threads, a job queue (Bull, Celery), or a dedicated compute service. Use async variants of crypto operations (pbkdf2, scrypt). Consider WebAssembly for hot-path computation.
Local filesystem path dependency: Use cloud storage (S3, Azure Blob, GCS) for persistent files. Use /tmp only for truly temporary data. Accept paths from environment configuration.

Console.log instead of structured logging: Use a structured logging library (pino/winston for JS, logging with dictConfig for Python, slog for Go, serilog for C#, log4j/slf4j for Java, tracing for Rust) that outputs JSON. Include correlation IDs, timestamps, and log levels.
Hardcoded connection string / service URL: Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) for all connection strings.
'any' type usage: Replace with specific types, generics, or constrained types. In TS enable 'noImplicitAny'. In Go use concrete types or type constraints.
Type-checker / linter error suppression: Fix the underlying issue instead of suppressing it. If suppression is truly necessary, add a comment explaining why.
TODO/FIXME/HACK comments found: Track TODOs as work items in your issue tracker. Resolve FIXMEs and HACKs before merging to main. Set a code quality gate that flags unresolved TODOs.
Bare except / untyped catch block: Catch specific exception types. In Python, use 'except ValueError' (not bare 'except:'). In Java/C#, catch specific exception classes.
Image missing alt attribute: Add meaningful alt text describing the image content. Use alt="" only for purely decorative images.
Click handler without keyboard equivalent: Add onKeyDown or onKeyPress handlers alongside onClick. Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible.
Non-semantic element used with ARIA role: Use semantic HTML elements (button, a, h1-h6, nav, main) instead of divs with ARIA roles.
Form input missing label association: Associate each input with a <label> element using for/id, or use aria-label / aria-labelledby.
Missing lang attribute on <html>: Add lang attribute: <html lang="en">. Use the appropriate BCP 47 language tag.
Focus indicator removed (outline: none): If removing outline, provide a visible alternative focus indicator (box-shadow, border, custom :focus-visible styles).
Form error not associated with input via ARIA: Use aria-describedby to link error messages to inputs, and aria-invalid='true' on invalid inputs.
Verb in REST endpoint URL: Use noun-based URLs (e.g., POST /users instead of POST /createUser). Let HTTP methods convey the action.
SELECT * in API handler: Explicitly select only the fields needed for the API response. Use DTOs or view models to shape the output.
List endpoint without pagination: Implement pagination using limit/offset, cursor-based, or page-based approaches. Include total count and navigation links.
No API versioning detected: Add API versioning via URL path (/v1/resource), header (X-API-Version), or query parameter.
Inconsistent API response structure: Adopt a consistent response envelope (e.g., { data, meta, errors }) across all endpoints.
Request body used without content-type validation: Use body-parsing middleware (express.json()) and validate Content-Type headers. Reject requests with unexpected content types.
Sensitive data in URL/query parameters: Pass sensitive data in request headers (Authorization) or request body, never in URLs or query parameters.
Network call without timeout: Set explicit timeouts on all network calls. Use AbortController with setTimeout for fetch, or timeout options for HTTP clients.
Abrupt process termination detected: Throw errors or use graceful shutdown patterns instead. Let the process exit naturally after cleanup. Reserve panics for truly unrecoverable situations.
No fallback for failed external call: Provide fallback behavior: cached responses, default values, or gracefully degraded features when dependencies fail.
Write endpoints without idempotency support: Accept an idempotency key header (Idempotency-Key) and use it to deduplicate write operations.

Console logging instead of structured logger: Use a structured logging library (winston/pino for JS, logging for Python, slog for Go, serilog for C#, log4j for Java, tracing for Rust) with log levels, timestamps, and correlation IDs.
String concatenation in log statements: Use structured log parameters: logger.info('User action', { userId, action }) instead of string concatenation.
Sensitive data potentially logged: Never log sensitive data. Use redaction middleware or mask sensitive fields before logging. Audit all log statements for PII/secrets.
Potential N+1 query pattern: Batch queries outside the loop using WHERE IN clauses, JOINs, or DataLoader patterns. Fetch all needed data in a single query.
Synchronous / blocking I/O detected: Use async/await versions, non-blocking APIs, or spawn blocking work on a separate thread/runtime. Sync I/O is only acceptable at startup.
Duplicate fetch calls to same URL: Cache responses or deduplicate requests. Use memoization, request coalescing, or an in-memory/distributed cache.
Heavy library imported eagerly: Use dynamic imports (import()) for heavy libraries. Import specific sub-modules (lodash/get instead of lodash). Consider tree-shakeable alternatives.
DOM manipulation inside loop: Build DOM content in a DocumentFragment or string, then insert once. Use virtual DOM frameworks or batch updates.
Unbounded data fetch without pagination: Always use pagination (limit/offset or cursor-based) when querying collections. Set reasonable default page sizes.

Nested loops detected — O(n²) or worse complexity: Replace nested loops with hash maps (O(n)) for lookups, pre-sorted data with binary search, or purpose-built data structures. Consider if the algorithm can be flattened.
setInterval without clearInterval — timer leak: Store the interval ID and call clearInterval in cleanup/teardown logic (e.g., useEffect cleanup, componentWillUnmount, or process exit handler).
PII field handled without protection: Encrypt PII fields, mask them in logs and UI displays, and ensure they are stored with column-level encryption.
Sensitive data in log statements: Never log sensitive data. Use redaction/masking utilities to sanitize log output. Audit all log statements.
Credit card data handling detected: Use a PCI-compliant payment processor (Stripe, Braintree). Never store, log, or transmit raw card numbers. Tokenize immediately.
Cookies set without security flags: Set Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Strict on sensitive cookies. Review cookie consent requirements per jurisdiction.
Potential cross-border data egress without jurisdiction checks: Add egress controls that validate destination jurisdiction, data classification, and lawful transfer conditions before sending data.
Replication/backup configuration may violate localization requirements: Pin replication and backup targets to approved jurisdictions and document DR geography constraints.

PII stored without geographic partitioning indicator: Tag PII records with a region/jurisdiction identifier. Use tenant-scoped region routing for multi-tenant systems. Implement database-level partitioning by geography for regulated data.

Administrative operations without audit trail: Log all administrative and destructive operations to a tamper-evident audit trail. Include actor identity, timestamp, operation type, affected resource, and outcome. Store audit logs in a separate, append-only store with retention policies.
TODO/FIXME without issue tracking reference: Link TODOs to issue tracker tickets (e.g., TODO(#123): ...). Create tracking issues for existing unlinked TODOs.
API endpoints without documentation: Add OpenAPI/Swagger annotations or JSDoc comments documenting request body, query params, response schema, and error codes.
Hardcoded user-facing strings: Use an i18n library (react-intl, i18next, vue-i18n) and extract strings to translation files.
String concatenation for user messages: Use parameterized translation strings with named placeholders: t('greeting', { name }) instead of 'Hello ' + name.
Hardcoded date or number format: Use Intl.DateTimeFormat and Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware formatting. Never hardcode date patterns.
Numeric values formatted without locale awareness: Use Intl.NumberFormat for all user-facing numbers: new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { style: 'currency', currency }).format(amount).
Deprecated or unmaintained package import: Replace deprecated packages: moment->date-fns/luxon, request->node-fetch/axios, underscore->lodash-es or native methods.
Shared mutable state in async context: Use request-scoped/context-scoped state, atomic operations, or proper synchronization mechanisms instead of shared mutable variables.
Potentially missing await on async operation: Add await to async operations, or explicitly handle the returned promise with .catch(). Use ESLint's no-floating-promises rule.
Sequential await in loop: For independent operations, collect promises and use Promise.all() (with concurrency limits). Keep sequential only if order matters.
setInterval without clearInterval: Store the interval ID and call clearInterval in cleanup/unmount/dispose handlers.
Demographic-based conditional logic: Review whether demographic-based logic is legally compliant and ethically justified. Document the business justification. Consider bias testing.
Non-inclusive language in code: Use inclusive alternatives: allowlist/denylist, primary/replica, placeholder, confidence check.
Weak or unsafe type usage detected: Replace weak types with specific types: use 'unknown' with type guards (TS), generics (Java/C#), concrete types (Go), safe wrappers (Rust).
Magic numbers detected: Extract magic numbers into named constants (e.g., const HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000). Use enums for related sets of values.
Technical debt markers (TODO/FIXME/HACK) found: Convert TODO/FIXME comments into tracked issues in your project management tool. Resolve HACK comments with proper implementations.
Functions with too many parameters: Use an options object parameter: func({ name, age, ...opts }). This is self-documenting, order-independent, and extensible.
Duplicate string literals — extract to constants: Extract repeated strings into named constants. This makes updates a single-point change and prevents typos.
Abrupt process termination instead of proper error handling: Use proper error propagation instead of abrupt termination. Return error responses in HTTP servers. Let the process shutdown gracefully.
Error responses without error codes: Include a machine-readable error code in responses: { code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message: '...' }. Use RFC 7807 Problem Details format.
Hardcoded credentials in source code: Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Never commit credentials to version control.
Sensitive tokens passed in query parameters: Pass tokens in the Authorization header (Bearer scheme) or in httpOnly cookies. Never use query parameters for sensitive credentials.
Weak hashing algorithm for credentials: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing. These algorithms are intentionally slow and include salt by default.
TLS certificate validation disabled: Never disable TLS verification in production. Fix certificate issues properly. Use CA bundles for self-signed certs in development only.
Cookies set without Secure/HttpOnly flags: Set cookies with { secure: true, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict' }. Use Secure for all auth cookies. HttpOnly prevents JavaScript access.
SQL injection via string concatenation: Use parameterized queries (placeholders) or prepared statements. ORMs handle this automatically. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.
SELECT * retrieves unnecessary columns: Select only the columns you need: SELECT id, name, email FROM users. This reduces network transfer, memory usage, and improves query plan optimization.
N+1 query pattern detected: Use batch queries (WHERE id IN (...)), JOINs, or ORM eager loading (include/populate) to fetch related data in a single query.
Data mutations without transaction handling: Wrap multi-step data mutations in transactions. Use BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK or ORM transaction APIs to ensure atomicity.
Hardcoded database connection string: Use environment variables for connection strings. Store credentials in a secrets manager. Use different connection strings per environment.

Destructive DDL statements in application code: Never run destructive DDL from application code. Use migration tools (Prisma, Flyway, Alembic) with review and rollback support. Require elevated permissions for DDL.

Database credentials embedded in connection string: Use separate credential parameters or environment variables. Consider IAM/managed identity for passwordless database connections in cloud environments.
Secrets hardcoded in source code: Store secrets in a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Inject via environment variables at runtime. Never commit secrets.

Configuration values hardcoded instead of externalized: Read configuration from environment variables (process.env.PORT). Use a config library (convict, dotenv, django-environ) to validate and provide defaults.
.env usage detected — ensure it is not committed: Add .env to .gitignore. Create a .env.example with placeholder values documenting required environment variables. Use CI/CD variables for deployment.
Multiple response formats — verify contract consistency: Use a consistent response envelope across all endpoints. Define response schemas (OpenAPI/Swagger) to enforce contracts.

HTTP method mismatch — destructive action via POST: Use appropriate HTTP methods: DELETE for removal, PUT/PATCH for updates. If migrating methods, keep the old method working during a deprecation period.
OS-specific file paths detected: Use platform-independent path construction (path.join, os.path.join, Path.Combine). Use environment variables or config for base directories.

File I/O without explicit line-ending handling: Use 'utf-8' encoding explicitly. Consider normalizing line endings when reading files. Configure .gitattributes for consistent line endings in version control.
OS-specific environment variables used directly: Use cross-platform helpers like os.homedir(), os.tmpdir(), or libraries like 'env-paths' to resolve platform-appropriate directories.
Browser-specific APIs used in server-side code: Guard browser API usage with typeof checks (e.g., typeof window !== 'undefined'). Use isomorphic libraries for code shared between client and server.
Inline event handlers in HTML: Use addEventListener() or framework event bindings (React onClick, Vue @click). Separate behavior from markup for maintainability and CSP compliance.
Form submission without loading/disabled state: Disable the submit button during submission. Show a loading indicator. Prevent double-submission at the application layer.
Destructive actions without confirmation: Add confirmation dialogs for destructive actions. Show what will be affected. Consider soft-delete with undo capability.
List endpoints without pagination: Implement pagination (offset-based or cursor-based). Return total count and page info. Enforce maximum page sizes.
Mutations without success feedback: Show success notifications (toasts, alerts) after mutations. Provide clear visual feedback. Consider optimistic UI updates with rollback on failure.

Form submission without client-side validation: Add client-side validation before submission. Use schema validation libraries (Zod, Yup, Joi). Show inline validation feedback. Keep server-side validation as well.
Authentication tokens logged: Never log authentication tokens, Authorization headers, or session IDs. If request logging is needed, redact sensitive headers before logging.
Passwords/secrets logged: Never log passwords, credentials, or secrets. Implement a log sanitizer that redacts sensitive fields automatically.

Unstructured logging lacks redaction capabilities: Use a structured logging library (pino, winston) that supports field-level redaction, log level filtering, and structured output for automated sensitivity scanning.
String concatenation in log statements: Use structured logging with named fields: logger.info({ userId, action }, 'User action performed'). This allows automated redaction of specific fields.

IP addresses logged without anonymization: Anonymize IP addresses in logs (truncate last octet for IPv4, mask prefix for IPv6). If full IP is needed for security, ensure log retention complies with privacy policy.

Database queries logged — may contain sensitive parameters: Log query templates without parameter values. Use parameterized query logging that replaces bind values with placeholders. Redact sensitive column values.
Unbounded query results without limit: Always enforce a maximum result limit: db.find({}).limit(100). Implement pagination and enforce maximum page sizes.
setInterval without rate control: Use setTimeout with re-scheduling instead of setInterval to prevent overlap. Add guards to skip execution if the previous run hasn't completed.

Authentication endpoints without rate limiting: Apply strict rate limits to auth endpoints (e.g., 5-10 requests/minute per IP). Use progressive delays or CAPTCHA after failed attempts. Consider using 'express-rate-limit' or 'rate-limiter-flexible'.
File upload without size or count limits: Set explicit file size limits (e.g., multer({ limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 } })). Limit the number of files per request. Validate file types.
Hard process termination hinders graceful CI/CD lifecycle: Use proper error propagation instead of hard exits. In production, handle SIGTERM gracefully. Let the runtime manage process lifecycle.

Static analysis suppression comments detected: Fix the underlying issues instead of suppressing them. If suppression is necessary, add a comment explaining why and create a tracking issue to resolve it.

Placeholder security comment — missing implementation: Implement the security controls indicated by each comment before merging. If the control is not needed, remove the comment and document why. Do not ship TODO security comments to production.

Type safety bypassed in security-critical code: Define proper interfaces for security-related data structures (tokens, sessions, credentials). Replace 'as any' with explicit types or runtime validation (zod, io-ts).
Hardcoded URLs or IP addresses: Move all endpoint URLs and IP addresses to environment variables or a configuration file. Use service discovery or DNS for internal services.

Request handlers without input validation: Add schema validation for all request inputs (body, query, params). Use zod, joi, or yup (Node.js), pydantic (Python), class-validator (NestJS), or equivalent for your framework. Validate at the boundary before any business logic.
Weak cryptographic hash (MD5/SHA-1): Replace MD5/SHA-1 with SHA-256+ for integrity checks, or bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for password hashing. Use crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data) in web contexts.

TLS certificate verification disabled: Never disable TLS verification in production. Use properly signed certificates (Let's Encrypt is free). If self-signed certificates are required for internal services, configure the specific CA certificate rather than disabling all verification.
Express error middleware registered before routes: Move error-handling middleware to after all route registrations: first register all routes, then app.use(errorHandler).
Express app without helmet() — missing security headers: Install and use helmet: npm install helmet, then app.use(helmet()). This sets 11 security headers with sensible defaults.
